tool.name: automate_login
tool.description:
Automate login to web application and extract authenticated session.
Performs automated authentication through various methods: - Simple form-based login (POST username/password) - JSON API authentication - Complex JavaScript-heavy login flows (using headless browser) - Multi-step authentication workflows
Automatically extracts and stores: - Session cookies (PHPSESSID, JSESSIONID, etc.) - CSRF tokens from login page - JWT tokens from API responses - Custom authentication headers
ELITE FEATURE: Use this to maintain authenticated context across all scanning operations. Many vulnerabilities only exist in authenticated surfaces.

parameters.parameter: String to check for successful login (e.g., "Dashboard", "Welcome", "Logout")
Name for the created session (default: auto-generated)
Use headless browser for JavaScript-heavy login (default: false)

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether login succeeded - session_id: Created session ID for authenticated requests - session_name: Session name - cookies: Extracted session cookies (redacted) - tokens: JWT or other auth tokens extracted - authenticated: True if login succeeded - login_duration_ms: Time taken for login flow - message: Status message
tool.example:
automate_login( target_url="https://target.com/login", username="admin", password="Password123", success_indicator="Dashboard" )
automate_login( target_url="https://api.target.com/auth/login", username="user@example.com", password="SecurePass456", flow_type="json_api" )
automate_login( target_url="https://spa.target.com/login", username="admin", password="test123", use_browser=true, success_indicator="Welcome back" )
tool.workflow: 1. Call automate_login() with target credentials 2. Tool fetches login page and extracts CSRF tokens 3. Submits credentials via POST request 4. Extracts session cookies and JWT tokens 5. Creates authenticated session automatically 6. Returns session_id for use with other tools 7. Use session_id with scanning tools to test authenticated surfaces

tool.name: refresh_jwt_token
tool.description:
Refresh expired JWT token to maintain authentication.
JWT tokens typically expire after 15-60 minutes. This tool automatically refreshes the access token using a refresh token.
ELITE FEATURE: During long scan operations, JWT tokens may expire. Use this tool to refresh tokens mid-scan and maintain access to protected endpoints.
Supports: - Automatic refresh using cached refresh token - Manual refresh with provided refresh token - Updates session with new access token - Caches new token for future refreshes

parameters.parameter: Session ID to refresh token for

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether token refresh succeeded - session_id: Updated session ID - access_token: New access token (redacted) - token_refreshed: True if successful - expires_in: Token expiration time (if provided by API) - message: Status message
tool.workflow: 1. Detect JWT expiration during scan (HTTP 401 responses) 2. Call refresh_jwt_token(session_id="...") 3. Tool POSTs to refresh endpoint with refresh token 4. Extracts new access token from response 5. Updates session Authorization header 6. Caches new token and refresh token 7. Resume scanning with refreshed authentication

tool.name: extract_jwt_from_response
tool.description:
Extract JWT tokens from HTTP response body and headers.
Searches for JWT tokens in: - JSON response bodies: {"token": "...", "access_token": "...", "id_token": "..."} - Authorization response headers: Authorization: Bearer ... - Refresh tokens: {"refresh_token": "..."}
Automatically parses token claims: - Issuer (iss) - Subject (sub) - Expiration (exp) - Issued at (iat)
ELITE FEATURE: Many modern web apps use JWT for authentication. This tool extracts tokens from API responses and validates their format before use.

parameters.parameter: Session to store extracted token in (optional)

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether any tokens were found - tokens: List of extracted tokens with parsed claims - token_count: Number of tokens found - primary_token: Most likely token with claims - refresh_tokens: List of refresh tokens found - stored_in_session: Session ID if token stored - message: Status message
tool.example:
extract_jwt_from_response( response_body='{"access_token":"eyJhbGc...", "refresh_token":"dGhpc2lz..."}' )
extract_jwt_from_response( response_body=api_response, response_headers={"Authorization": "Bearer eyJhbGc..."}, session_id="abc12345" )
tool.workflow: 1. Perform API authentication request 2. Call extract_jwt_from_response() on response 3. Tool parses JWT and validates format 4. Extracts token claims (expiration, subject, etc.) 5. Stores token in session if session_id provided 6. Use extracted token for authenticated API requests

tool.name: check_jwt_expiration
tool.description:
Check JWT token expiration status and validity.
Parses JWT token to determine: - Is token expired? - Is token expiring soon? (within 5 minutes) - Time remaining until expiration - Token claims (issuer, subject, etc.)
ELITE FEATURE: Proactively check token expiration before making critical requests. Prevents authentication failures mid-scan by refreshing tokens before they expire.

parameters.parameter: Session ID to check cached token (alternative to token parameter)

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether check completed - has_expiration: Whether token has exp claim - is_expired: True if token is expired - expires_soon: True if expiring within 5 minutes - expires_at: ISO timestamp of expiration - time_remaining_seconds: Seconds until expiration - claims: Full JWT claims - recommendation: Action to take (refresh, valid, etc.)
tool.workflow: 1. Before critical authenticated request, check token expiration 2. Call check_jwt_expiration(session_id="...") 3. If is_expired or expires_soon, call refresh_jwt_token() 4. Proceed with request using valid token

tool.name: multi_step_login
tool.description:
Execute complex multi-step authentication flows.
Handles authentication requiring multiple sequential requests: - Multi-page login forms - Two-factor authentication (2FA) - OAuth2 authorization code flow - SAML SSO workflows - CSRF token extraction + login + redirect
ELITE FEATURE: Modern enterprise apps often have complex auth. This tool chains requests together, extracting tokens/cookies at each step and passing them to subsequent steps.
Each step can: - GET or POST to URLs - Extract CSRF tokens - Extract JWT tokens - Parse and store cookies - Inject data from previous steps - Check for success/failure indicators

parameters.parameter: List of step configurations. Each step is a dict with: - type: "get" or "post" - url: Request URL - data: Form/JSON data (for POST) - extract_csrf: Extract CSRF token (bool) - extract_jwt: Extract JWT token (bool) - inject_csrf: Inject CSRF into form (bool) - csrf_field: Field name for CSRF (default: "csrf_token") - content_type: "form" or "json" - failure_indicators: List of strings indicating failure
Name for the created session (default: auto-generated)

tool.returns: JSON object containing: - success: Whether all steps completed successfully - session_id: Created session ID - session_name: Session name - cookies: Final cookies (redacted) - headers: Final headers (redacted) - csrf_token: Final CSRF token (redacted) - steps_executed: Number of steps completed - step_results: Results from each step - duration_ms: Total flow duration - message: Status message
tool.workflow: 1. Define authentication steps as list of dicts 2. Call multi_step_login(steps=[...]) 3. Tool executes steps sequentially 4. Each step can extract/inject CSRF, JWT, cookies 5. Cookies and tokens accumulated across steps 6. Final session created with all auth data 7. Use session_id for authenticated scanning
